title: Fix GPS capture and camera/photo button in AssetEntryForm

requirements.text: Fix the camera/photo capture button in AssetEntryForm.tsx so that tapping 'Take Photo' or 'Upload from Gallery' correctly opens the device camera or file picker on mobile devices. The PhotoUpload component's file input must have the correct `accept` and `capture` attributes, and any event handler wiring issues preventing the button from triggering the input must be resolved.

quotes: camera button is not working pls correct error

acceptanceCriteria: Tapping the camera button on a mobile device opens the device camera to capture a photo.
Tapping the gallery/upload button opens the device file picker to select an image.
Selected or captured photos appear in the photo preview grid within AssetEntryForm.
The form cannot be submitted without at least one photo attached (existing validation preserved).
No JavaScript errors are thrown when interacting with the photo capture/upload controls.

requirements.text: Fix the GPS capture flow in AssetEntryForm.tsx so that GPS coordinates are reliably obtained on form submission. The `useGeolocation` hook's `capture` function must be called and awaited before building the submission payload, and the resolved latitude, longitude, and accuracy values must be read from the promise result (not from stale React state) and correctly included in the asset record sent to the backend.

quotes: GPS and camera button is not working pls correct error

acceptanceCriteria: On form submission, the browser prompts the user for location permission if not already granted.
After permission is granted, GPS coordinates (latitude and longitude) are captured and attached to the submission payload.
The submitted asset record stored in the backend contains non-null, accurate GPS latitude and longitude values.
GPS coordinates are displayed on the submission confirmation and in the Admin Dashboard detail view.
If GPS capture fails (e.g., permission denied), a clear error message is shown to the user and submission is blocked or proceeds with a graceful fallback, consistent with existing UX.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not alter the backend data model or Motoko actor unless strictly required to fix the GPS/photo issues.
Preserve all existing form validation logic (mandatory photo, required fields).
nonGoals: Adding new form fields or changing the asset data schema.
Redesigning the visual theme or layout.
Changes to the Admin Dashboard beyond what is needed to display correctly captured GPS data.
